description: When user selects a node to open in VS Code, vscode.OpenInVSCode currently opens the folder path directly. Check if a .code-workspace file exists in the directory first. If found, prefer opening that workspace file over the folder
acceptance_criteria: Check for *.code-workspace files in the target directory before opening
If a .code-workspace file exists, pass that to 'code' command instead of directory
If multiple .code-workspace files exist, choose deterministically (e.g., alphabetically first or shortest name)
If no .code-workspace file exists, fall back to current behavior (open directory)
Handle errors gracefully if workspace file exists but can't be read
